sources.introductionYear: cinematechnic.com: Master Prime series launched NAB 2005; Master Zoom introduced contemporaneously
sources.imageCircleMm: ARRI Master Prime Lenses brochure PDF (arri.com, Tier 1) -- ANSI Super 35 ID = 31.14mm
sources.filterType: arri.com matte box rings page: no filter thread; 165mm is barrel diameter — matte box use only
sources.isParfocal: POS-D11: ARRI/Zeiss claims 'zero lens breathing' (focus breathing) and 'field of view stable while focusing' but does not claim parfocal; absence of parfocal claim is sufficient per POS-D11

sources.hasFrontRotation: Internal focus design; arri.com Master Zoom product page
sources.gearPitch: ARRI follow-focus gear specs: 0.8 module standard for ARRI Master class zoom lenses
sources.focusRingRotationDeg: ARRI/ZEISS Master Zoom technical data -- arri.com (Tier 1)

FD Times 2014-04-05: 'ARRI Ultra Wide Zoom 9.5-18mm'; began shipping April-May 2014, announced IBC 2013
sources.imageCircleMm: arri.com UWZ product page + FD Times 2014: 34.5mm image circle
sources.weightG: arri.com UWZ technical data
sources.lengthMm: arri.com UWZ technical data: 335.5mm mechanical length
sources.frontDiameterMm: arri.com UWZ technical data: 134mm standard matte box interface (156mm accessory ring available)
sources.closeFocusM: arri.com UWZ technical data: 0.55m from film plane
sources.filterType: arri.com UWZ product page: exchangeable matte box interfaces (134mm and 156mm); no front thread
sources.hasFocusBreathing: arri.com UWZ product page: 'virtually no image breathing when focusing'
sources.isParfocal: POS-D11: arri.com 'fixed entrance pupil' language describes focus breathing behaviour, not parfocal; no parfocal claim made by ARRI
sources.gearPitch: ARRI CLM-5 accessories doc confirms m0.8/32p gear compatible with all ARRI/Zeiss and Fujifilm focus, iris, and zoom gears. No explicit UWZ data sheet statement found.
sources.focusRingRotationDeg: ARRI Ultra Wide Zoom documentation -- arri.com (Tier 1)
